variants.setup:
mkdir -p /tmp/eval_pal && printf 'A man a plan a canal Panama
racecar
hello

Was it a car or a cat I saw?
No lemon, no melon
' > /tmp/eval_pal/input.txt && printf 'true
true
false
true
true
true
' > /tmp/eval_pal/expected.txt
variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_pal/pal.py — a Python program that reads lines from stdin and for each line writes 'true' (lowercase) on its own line if the line is a palindrome considering only alphanumeric characters (case-insensitive), or 'false' otherwise. The empty line is a palindrome (true).

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_pal && python3 pal.py < input.txt > out.txt && diff -u expected.txt out.txt && echo OK

variants.setup:
mkdir -p /tmp/eval_pal && printf 'A man a plan a canal Panama
racecar
hello

Was it a car or a cat I saw?
No lemon, no melon
' > /tmp/eval_pal/input.txt && printf 'true
true
false
true
true
true
' > /tmp/eval_pal/expected.txt
variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_pal/pal.go (package main) that reads lines from stdin and for each line writes 'true' (lowercase) on its own line if the line is a palindrome considering only alphanumeric characters (case-insensitive), or 'false' otherwise. The empty line is a palindrome (true). Compile with `go build -o pal pal.go`.

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_pal && go build -o pal pal.go && ./pal < input.txt > out.txt && diff -u expected.txt out.txt && echo OK

variants.setup:
mkdir -p /tmp/eval_pal && printf 'A man a plan a canal Panama
racecar
hello

Was it a car or a cat I saw?
No lemon, no melon
' > /tmp/eval_pal/input.txt && printf 'true
true
false
true
true
true
' > /tmp/eval_pal/expected.txt
variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_pal/pal.c — a C program that reads lines from stdin (use fgets or getline; lines may have any length up to 4096) and for each one writes 'true' or 'false' (lowercase) on its own line. A line is a palindrome if its alphanumeric characters (case-insensitive) read the same forward and backward. The empty line is a palindrome. Must compile cleanly with `gcc -O2 -std=c11 -Wall -Wextra -o pal pal.c`.

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_pal && gcc -O2 -std=c11 -Wall -Wextra -o pal pal.c && ./pal < input.txt > out.txt && diff -u expected.txt out.txt && echo OK

variants.setup:
mkdir -p /tmp/eval_pal && printf 'A man a plan a canal Panama
racecar
hello

Was it a car or a cat I saw?
No lemon, no melon
' > /tmp/eval_pal/input.txt && printf 'true
true
false
true
true
true
' > /tmp/eval_pal/expected.txt
variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_pal/pal.cpp — a C++ program that reads lines from stdin (std::getline) and for each one writes 'true' or 'false' (lowercase) on its own line. A line is a palindrome if its alphanumeric characters (case-insensitive) read the same forward and backward. The empty line is a palindrome. Must compile cleanly with `g++ -O2 -std=c++17 -Wall -Wextra -o pal pal.cpp`.

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_pal && g++ -O2 -std=c++17 -Wall -Wextra -o pal pal.cpp && ./pal < input.txt > out.txt && diff -u expected.txt out.txt && echo OK
